channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: [6] Detecting linear dichroism with atomic resolution
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 12 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02606-6

An electron linear dichroism method uses electron energy loss spectroscopy with an atomic-sized probe and momentum transfer selection along two orthogonal directions to resolve orbital occupation at individual atomic columns in real space.
item.dc:title: [6] Detecting linear dichroism with atomic resolution
item.dc:creator: Roger Guzman, Ján Rusz, Ang Li, Juan Carlos Idrobo, Wu Zhou, Jaume Gazquez
Ján Rusz
Ang Li
Juan Carlos Idrobo
Wu Zhou
Jaume Gazquez
item.description:
Last authors: Wu Zhou, Jaume Gazquez

Authors: Roger Guzman, Ján Rusz, Ang Li, Juan Carlos Idrobo, Wu Zhou, Jaume Gazquez

Score 6/10 C_MAYBE

Why: New methodology for orbital resolution at atomic scale, potentially useful for future spectroscopy experiments.

Tags: #spectroscopy #orbital #microscopy

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 12 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02606-6 An electron linear dichroism method uses electron energy loss spectroscopy with an atomic-sized probe and momentum transfer selection along two orthogonal directions to resolve orbital occupation at individual atomic columns in real space.

item.title: [7] Two-dimensional magnets stack up against multiferroic competition
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02591-w

Bilayer 1T-CrTe2 breaks time-reversal and inversion symmetry in its magnetic state, allowing for strong magnetoelectric coupling at room temperature.
item.dc:title: [7] Two-dimensional magnets stack up against multiferroic competition
item.dc:creator: Meixin Cheng, Adam W. Tsen
Adam W. Tsen
item.description:
Last authors: Meixin Cheng, Adam W. Tsen

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of 2D magnetic systems with symmetry breaking, relevant to topological and magnetic materials interest.

Tags: #2D #magnetism #multiferroic

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 06 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02591-w Bilayer 1T-CrTe2 breaks time-reversal and inversion symmetry in its magnetic state, allowing for strong magnetoelectric coupling at room temperature.

item.title: [6] Observation of coherent ferron emission and propagation
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 04 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02597-4

Coherent ferrons (or polarization waves) driven by an ultrafast laser source in ferroelectric van der Waals materials generate narrow-band terahertz radiation and propagate uniaxially with long coherence times.
item.dc:title: [6] Observation of coherent ferron emission and propagation
item.dc:creator: Jeongheon Choe, Taketo Handa, Chun-Ying Huang, et al.
Taketo Handa
Chun-Ying Huang
André Koch Liston
Jordan Cox
Jonathan Stensberg
Yongseok Hong
Daniel G. Chica
Ding Xu
Fuyang Tay
Samra Husremovic
Vinicius da Silveira Lanza Avelar
Eric A. Arsenault
Zhuquan Zhang
James McIver
Dmitri N. Basov
Milan Delor
Xavier Roy
X.-Y. Zhu
item.description:
Last authors: Xavier Roy, X.-Y. Zhu

Authors: Jeongheon Choe, Taketo Handa, Chun-Ying Huang, et al.

Score 6/10 C_MAYBE

Why: Ultrafast dynamics in van der Waals ferroelectrics are relevant to experimental quantum materials physics.

Tags: #vanderWaals #terahertz #dynamics

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 04 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02597-4 Coherent ferrons (or polarization waves) driven by an ultrafast laser source in ferroelectric van der Waals materials generate narrow-band terahertz radiation and propagate uniaxially with long coherence times.

item.title: [8] Interface effects unlock unusual superconductivity in a light-element superconductor
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02605-7

A heterostructure comprising a trilayer of gallium, sandwiched between graphene and silicon carbide, exhibits Ising-type superconductivity driven by quantum confinement and interfacial orbital hybridization. This light-element superconductor retains its superconductivity under in-plane magnetic fields three times larger than the Pauli paramagnetic limit of conventional superconductors.
item.dc:title: [8] Interface effects unlock unusual superconductivity in a light-element superconductor
item.description:
Authors: Authors not available in source RSS

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Interesting Ising-type superconductivity in heterostructures, aligns with interest in unconventional superconductors and vdW systems.

Tags: #superconductivity #graphene #heterostructure

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02605-7 A heterostructure comprising a trilayer of gallium, sandwiched between graphene and silicon carbide, exhibits Ising-type superconductivity driven by quantum confinement and interfacial orbital hybridization. This light-element superconductor retains its superconductivity under in-plane magnetic fields three times larger than the Pauli paramagnetic limit of conventional superconductors.

item.title: [8] Field-resolved observation of exciton coherence in a van der Waals magnet
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02598-3

Coherent exciton dynamics in antiferromagnetic CrSBr are studied by petahertz field-resolved spectroscopy, revealing how laser-imprinted exciton coherence persists beyond excitation and is modulated by spin and lattice modes.
item.dc:title: [8] Field-resolved observation of exciton coherence in a van der Waals magnet
item.dc:creator: Matthew Yeung, Alexander von Hoegen, Emil Viñas Boström, et al.
Alexander von Hoegen
Emil Viñas Boström
Fangzhou Zhao
Felix Ritzkowsky
Jack B. Maier
Gian Luca Dolso
Christian Heide
Daniel G. Chica
Xavier Roy
Karl K. Berggren
Angel Rubio
Phillip D. Keathley
Nuh Gedik
item.description:
Last authors: Phillip D. Keathley, Nuh Gedik

Authors: Matthew Yeung, Alexander von Hoegen, Emil Viñas Boström, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: High-impact study on exciton-spin coupling in vdW antiferromagnets, highly relevant to quantum magnets.

Tags: #vanderWaals #magnetism #spectroscopy

Abstract: Nature Materials, Published online: 01 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-026-02598-3 Coherent exciton dynamics in antiferromagnetic CrSBr are studied by petahertz field-resolved spectroscopy, revealing how laser-imprinted exciton coherence persists beyond excitation and is modulated by spin and lattice modes.
